mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the industrial production index reflects contracting manufacturing output that reduces aggregate corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative during months when INDPRO declines, specifically if the mean return is greater than or equal to zero or the t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% triggers a repricing of recession risk as labor market slack exceeds the natural rate, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of an elevated federal funds rate above 5% and a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index raises expectations of additional tightening, which increases equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL returns a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 20-day returns, or the signal produces positive mean returns during active trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal consumer demand exhaustion that precedes revenue misses and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, months with declining RSAFS fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls above its trailing 12-month trend reflects accelerating employment growth that raises near-term aggregate demand expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with accelerating PAYEMS growth do not produce positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production growth alleviates supply constraints and lowers unit cost pressures, which expands profit margin expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of accelerating INDPRO fail to generate positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months, or the mean excess return is not statistically positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment that drags construction employment and durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances with HOUST below 1400 produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the cumulative return distribution is not significantly below zero (Wilcoxon p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payrolls over two consecutive months confirms a negative employment trend that reduces household income growth expectations and triggers persistent risk-off positioning, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive months of declining PAYEMS are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the median return is not negative.
